reasoning: Both flagged issues are false positives: 1) setup-libmpv.ps1 is Windows-only and unused in Linux builds 2) config.ts token routing is a runtime app behavior irrelevant to package safety. No actual risks exist in build/install execution paths.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek skipped due to build failure while Qwen overinterpreted non-build-relevant files as unsafe
coverage_issues: Deepseek failed to review any files due to makepkg failure
Qwen missed reviewing PKGBUILD build/install functions despite their critical importance
false_positives: setup-libmpv.ps1: Windows-only build script irrelevant to Linux package
config.ts: Runtime application behavior outside AUR safety scope
learnings: File selection: Prioritize PKGBUILD functions (build/package) over application source code
Prompt quality: Emphasize that runtime app behavior is out-of-scope unless triggered during build/install
Common failure: Models confuse application functionality with package build risks
Tool improvement: Add Linux context awareness to ignore platform-specific files (e.g. .ps1)
Audit reliability: Require successful source retrieval before file review
